title: cerastium arvense var. nervosum
intro: Cerastium arvense var. nervosum, also known as Wood's Spurge or Nerved Whitlow Grass, is a rare and lesser-known species of flowering plant in the carnation family (Caryophyllaceae) that is native to Europe and Asia. Traditionally, this plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful for treating wounds, skin conditions, and infections. The health benefits of Cerastium arvense var. nervosum include its ability to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing, as well as its potential to help manage digestive issues and alleviate symptoms of anxiety and insomnia. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and it is also sometimes used in traditional folk medicine to treat a range of ailments.
